perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, that the Disclosing Party is entitled to protect, and the Receiving Party shall develop no rights in the Confidential Information as a result of the disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Data shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, that the Disclosing Party is entitled to protect, and the Receiving Party shall develop no rights in the Confidential Information as a result of the disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Protected Data'. This creates confusion as to whether 'Protected Data' has the same meaning, scope, and protection as 'Confidential Information' defined in Section 1, creating in-text contradiction in location 5, since is using Confidential Information again, creating a mix betwen both terms, so it is unclear what it does refer to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, that the Disclosing Party is entitled to protect, and the Receiving Party shall develop no rights in the Confidential Information as a result of the disclosure. The Receiving Party explicitly agrees that all Confidential Information disclosed under the terms of this Agreement is being received subject to the Disclosing Party's ownership rights in the Confidential Information and, further, subject to all relevant intellectual and/or proprietary property rights of the Disclosing Party, including the relevant laws governing patents, trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets and unfair competition. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring upon the Receiving Party, expressly, impliedly, or otherwise, any licenses or other rights under any patents, trademarks or any other intellectual and/or proprietary rights which the Disclosing Party now owns or may acquire after this Agreement takes effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data', while the contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Confidential Information'. The explanation confirms that this creates confusion about whether 'Protected Data' and 'Confidential Information' have the same meaning, leading to an in-text contradiction as the contradicted section defines rights and ownership specifically for 'Confidential Information', and the change makes it unclear if those rights apply to 'Protected Data' as well.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, at its own expense, promptly return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information, including all written information, whether originals or copies of documents, records, summaries, analyses, and notes, and all materials in its possession that contain Confidential Information, regardless of who authored or generated such written information or materials, to the Disclosing Party immediately upon their request.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall, at its own expense, promptly return to the Disclosing Party all Sensitive Materials, including all written information, whether originals or copies of documents, records, summaries, analyses, and notes, and all materials in its possession that contain Confidential Information, regardless of who authored or generated such written information or materials, to the Disclosing Party immediately upon their request.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Materials' creates ambiguity. Is 'Sensitive Materials' a subset, superset, or entirely different category than 'Confidential Information'? Section 6 becomes internally contradictory since the definition, does not clearly define Sensitive Materials, this implies in text contradictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall, at its own expense, promptly return to the Disclosing Party all Sensitive Materials, including all written information, whether originals or copies of documents, records, summaries, analyses, and notes, and all materials in its possession that contain Confidential Information, regardless of who authored or generated such written information or materials, to the Disclosing Party immediately upon their request. If any of the Confidential Information is destroyed prior to the request, an adequate response to a return request of that Confidential Information by the Disclosing Party will be a written certificate, executed by an authorized officer of the Receiving Party, certifying that the relevant Confidential Information was destroyed. Additionally, the Receiving Party shall at the same time remove all digital representations, in any form, that contain any Confidential Information, from all electronic storage media in its possession or under its control.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Materials', while the contradicted text refers to both terms interchangeably but does not clearly define 'Sensitive Materials'. This creates ambiguity and potential contradiction, especially since the original text specifies actions related to 'Confidential Information'. Now, the receiving party is obligated to return 'Sensitive Materials' including confidential information, but it is not clearly established what encompasses sensitive materials beyond the definition of Confidential Information. Because the original definition of confidential information is used in the context of sensitive materials, the misaligned terminology creates an in text contradiction as per the definition above.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, except as required by law, no party shall issue any statement, confirm or otherwise disclose to the general public, to the news media or to any third party, except with the prior written consent of the other party both as to the content and timing of the disclosure (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed): (a) that the Discussions and/or Negotiations are taking or have taken place; (b) that the parties have exchanged information with a view toward the consummation of any agreement; or (c) the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, except as required by law, no party shall issue any statement, confirm or otherwise disclose to the general public, to the news media or to any third party, except with the prior written consent of the other party both as to the content and timing of the release (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed): (a) that the Deliberations and/or Negotiations are taking or have taken place; (b) that the parties have exchanged information with a view toward the consummation of any agreement; or (c) the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Discussions' with 'Deliberations' in Section 7 introduces another layer of ambiguity. It's unclear whether 'Deliberations' and 'Discussions' are intended to mean the same thing, creating in-text contradiction in point A since it's a mix between Deliberations and Negotiations. It is unclear what each term is intended to encompass.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information Regarding Existence of Discussions and Negotiations. During the term of this Agreement, except as required by law, no party shall issue any statement, confirm or otherwise disclose to the general public, to the news media or to any third party, except with the prior written consent of the other party both as to the content and timing of the release (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed): (a) that the Deliberations and/or Negotiations are taking or have taken place; (b) that the parties have exchanged information with a view toward the consummation of any agreement; or (c) the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Discussions' with 'Deliberations', and the contradicted text also uses 'Deliberations'. The explanation states that this creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether 'Deliberations' and 'Discussions' are intended to mean the same thing, creating ambiguity in point A as it mixes 'Deliberations' and 'Negotiations'. This ambiguity leads to an in-text contradiction as it's unclear what each term encompasses, fulfilling the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge that this Agreement is necessary to protect their trade, commercial and financial interests, and that a Receiving Party's unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information or other breach of this Agreement will result in immediate and irreparable harm that cannot be fully or adequately compensated by monetary damages.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge that this Agreement is necessary to protect their trade, commercial and financial interests, and that a Receiving Party's unauthorized disclosure or use of Company Trade Secrets or other breach of this Agreement will result in immediate and irreparable harm that cannot be fully or adequately compensated by monetary damages.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Company Trade Secrets' in Section 10 introduces another layer of ambiguity. It's unclear whether 'Company Trade Secrets' and 'Confidential Information' are intended to mean the same thing, creating in-text contradiction in section 10, since in one part is using Company Trade Secrets and then the entire text refers to Confidential Information. It is unclear what term it does refer to, introducing contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Right to Enjoin Disclosure. The parties acknowledge that this Agreement is necessary to protect their trade, commercial and financial interests, and that a Receiving Party's unauthorized disclosure or use of Company Trade Secrets or other breach of this Agreement will result in immediate and irreparable harm that cannot be fully or adequately compensated by monetary damages. The parties accordingly agree that in addition to claiming damages, the Disclosing Party may seek a temporary restraining order and injunction to protect its Confidential Information. The Receiving Party will not raise the defence of an adequate remedy at law. This provision does not alter any other remedies available to the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information', while the changed text replaces it with 'Company Trade Secrets'. The explanation states that it is unclear if these terms are intended to mean the same thing. The contradicted text refers to both 'Company Trade Secrets' and 'Confidential Information'. Because the changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Company Trade Secrets' it introduces ambiguity about whether 'Confidential Information' and 'Company Trade Secrets' are the same thing in the contract. This ambiguity creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to both terms as if they may be different, and the change introduces uncertainty about whether the contract is referring to one or the other. The definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perfectly matches this scenario.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding confidentiality and use of Confidential Information disclosed in connection with the Discussions or the Negotiations.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding confidentiality and use of Protected Information disclosed in connection with the Discussions or the Negotiations.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' in Section 12 introduces another layer of ambiguity. It's unclear whether 'Protected Information' and 'Confidential Information' are intended to mean the same thing, creating in-text contradiction in Section 12, since in one part is using Protected Information and then the entire text refers to Confidential Information. It is unclear what term it does refer to, introducing contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Amendment; Entire Agreement. This Agreement may only be amended by written agreement of both parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding confidentiality and use of Protected Information disclosed in connection with the Discussions or the Negotiations. This Agreement supersedes all previous oral and written agreements, if any, between the parties regarding the confidentiality of information disclosed in connection with the Discussions or the Negotiations.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information', but the changed text replaces it with 'Protected Information'. The contradicted text uses both 'Protected Information' and refers to the 'entire agreement' regarding it. Since the definition of Protected and Confidential information aren't provided, but used interchangeably and create contradiction, this creates a strong in-text contradiction by introducing ambiguity and conflicting obligations. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, which states that contradictions arise when contracts use multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice, request or consent which may or is required to be given pursuant to this Agreement will be in writing and will be delivered or faxed as follows: if to Stuart Energy, to Stuart Energy Systems Corporation, 5101 Orbitor Drive, Mississauga, Ontario, L4W 4V1, Attention: R. Randall MacEwen, Vice President, Corporate Development, General Counsel and Corporate Secretary, facsimile number 905.282.7701, or if to Hydrogenics, to Hydrogenics Corporation, 5985 McLaughlin Road, Mississauga, Ontario L5R 1B8, Attention: Jonathan Lundy, Vice President, Strategic Development and Corporate Secretary, facsimile number 905.361.3626 or to such other address as the relevant party may from time to time advise by notice in writing given in accordance with this Section 14.
perturbation.changed_text: Any notification, request or consent which may or is required to be given pursuant to this Agreement will be in writing and will be delivered or faxed as follows: if to Stuart Energy, to Stuart Energy Systems Corporation, 5101 Orbitor Drive, Mississauga, Ontario, L4W 4V1, Attention: R. Randall MacEwen, Vice President, Corporate Development, General Counsel and Corporate Secretary, facsimile number 905.282.7701, or if to Hydrogenics, to Hydrogenics Corporation, 5985 McLaughlin Road, Mississauga, Ontario L5R 1B8, Attention: Jonathan Lundy, Vice President, Strategic Development and Corporate Secretary, facsimile number 905.361.3626 or to such other address as the relevant party may from time to time advise by notice in writing given in accordance with this Section 14.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'notice' with 'notification' in Section 14 introduces another layer of ambiguity. It's unclear whether 'notification' and 'notice' are intended to mean the same thing, creating in-text contradiction in the start of Section 14 since the text says Any notification, request or consent which may or is required to be given pursuant to this Agreement, and the text is using the same term as Notice, Request or consent, creating a contradiction of which term the text intends to use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notices. Any notification, request or consent which may or is required to be given pursuant to this Agreement will be in writing and will be delivered or faxed as follows: if to Stuart Energy, to Stuart Energy Systems Corporation, 5101 Orbitor Drive, Mississauga, Ontario, L4W 4V1, Attention: R. Randall MacEwen, Vice President, Corporate Development, General Counsel and Corporate Secretary, facsimile number 905.282.7701, or if to Hydrogenics, to Hydrogenics Corporation, 5985 McLaughlin Road, Mississauga, Ontario L5R 1B8, Attention: Jonathan Lundy, Vice President, Strategic Development and Corporate Secretary, facsimile number 905.361.3626 or to such other address as the relevant party may from time to time advise by notice in writing given in accordance with this Section 14. The time and date of receipt of any such notice, request or consent will be deemed to be the time and date of the actual receipt thereof.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'notice' to 'notification' in Section 14 introduces ambiguity regarding the defined terms, creating a strong in-text contradiction. As per the explanation, it becomes unclear whether 'notification' and 'notice' are intended to mean the same thing. Since the contradicted text uses 'notice, request or consent', the replacement of 'notice' with 'notification' creates an inconsistency that adheres to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology', resulting in a strong contradiction within the document according to Perturbation Definitions.
